venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

market_positions.selection: No - San Antonio wins by over 3.5 points

quarter_flow.note: Tight open. Wembanyama rim protection appeared immediately; Minnesota still found enough paint and transition recovery.
Half ended tied 45-45. Pace and scoring stayed suppressed, validating low-total lean through halftime.
San Antonio led 72-69 entering the 4Q; spread-fade setup became attractive because game remained one-possession density.
Edwards returned as closer, Minnesota shot 63.2% in Q4 and flipped the result. Total landed 206, burning Under/No over 205.5 by a hook.

successful_reads: Randle rebound funnel was correctly identified. He logged 41:28 and reached 10 rebounds, clearing both 7+ and 8+.
San Antonio margin fade was correct. Spurs did not win by 4+; Minnesota won outright by 2.
LiveFlow recognized one-possession game texture and resisted overpricing Spurs control.
misses: No over 205.5 lost by 0.5 point as the final landed 206.
Fourth-quarter Edwards scoring return and late efficiency created enough points to break the low-total thesis by the hook.

rebound_funnel_review.reason_for_hit: Randle played 41:28, generated 5 offensive rebounds and 5 defensive rebounds, and remained central because Minnesota needed his size/usage against Wembanyama. High Wemby block volume extended miss/rebound sequences rather than suppressing Randle boards.
rebound_funnel_review.future_rule: When Randle projects 38+ minutes in a physical playoff matchup, his 7+/8+ rebound ladders remain live if opponent rim contests create extra rebound events and blowout risk is low.

edge_scoring.notes: Excellent micro-stake LiveFlow sequence. Only miss was a 0.5-point total hook, while both Randle rebound ladders and Spurs margin fade hit.

source_notes: Official NBA scorer report uploaded as 20260504_MINSAS_book (2).pdf
